content.text: Tayammum is a permitted alternative to wudu or ghusl when water is unavailable, harmful, or its use is impossible.

items: No water available or using it would cause harm (illness, wound).
Water is needed for other essential uses (e.g., drinking) and scarce.
Intend (niyya) to perform tayammum for purification.
Strike both hands lightly on clean earth, dust, stone or similar natural surface.
Brush the face with both hands.
Brush both hands up to the wrists.

content.text: Tayammum is valid only while water remains unavailable. When water becomes available, it must be used for future prayers.

items: Deliberate avoidance of water without valid reason.

content.text: Tayammum shows Islam's mercy — concessions exist when hardship occurs.
